error.signature: AI ignores Middle Eastern customs around food, greetings, and religious observance

verdict.summary: Middle Eastern and Islamic cultures have specific customs around dining, greetings, religious observance, and social interaction that AI must respect in recommendations.

dead_ends.action: Suggest scheduling business meals during Ramadan fasting hours
dead_ends.why_fails: During Ramadan, Muslims fast from dawn to sunset. Scheduling lunch meetings or food-related events during daylight hours shows ignorance and disrespect.
dead_ends.condition: Scheduling in Muslim-majority regions during Ramadan

dead_ends.action: Recommend non-halal food or alcohol in business contexts
dead_ends.why_fails: Alcohol is haram in Islam. Suggesting drinks at a bar or non-halal restaurants for business entertainment is offensive. Always verify dietary requirements.
dead_ends.condition: Restaurant or event recommendations for Middle Eastern clients

dead_ends.action: Suggest left-hand gestures for greetings or passing items
dead_ends.why_fails: In many Middle Eastern and South Asian cultures, the left hand is considered unclean. Passing objects, eating, or gesturing with the left hand is offensive.
dead_ends.condition: Physical interaction advice in Middle Eastern context

workarounds.action: Check Islamic calendar for Ramadan dates and schedule meetings after Iftar (sunset meal). Respect Friday as the holy day (equivalent to Sunday in Western culture)
Default to halal-certified restaurants for business dining. Avoid suggesting alcohol; offer Arabic coffee (قهوة عربية) or tea as hospitality default
workarounds.condition: Business entertainment in Middle Eastern context

workarounds.action: In greeting advice, emphasize right-hand usage. Note that handshakes between unrelated men and women may not be appropriate; let the other party initiate
